 based automation triggers on 196.\",\n    \"UID/GID mapping mismatches between

ding to potential read/write permission errors.\",\n    \"Risk of database/state
 corruption if Docker volumes for stateful services (e.g., MySQL, Redis, SQLite)
 are accidentally mapped to the NFS share instead of local 196 storage.\"\n  ],\
n  \"reasoning\": \"While NFS provides immediate file synchronization, it create
s significant friction with Docker and automated deployments. The weakest assump
tion in the initial framing is that automation can seamlessly trigger off file c
hanges across an NFS mount. Because `inotify` events fail over NFS, we cannot re
ly on standard 'on-change' triggers. We must explicitly define how the 196 machi

 Therefore, 196 cannot automatically detect when 199 updates the code. To achiev

onally, UID/GID alignment between 199 and 196 is critical to ensure Docker conta
iners on 196 have the correct read/write permissions for the mounted NFS volumes
